title: Study of RMC-5127 in Patients With Advanced KRAS G12V-Mutant Solid Tumors

eligibility.text: At least 18 years old and has provided informed consent.
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures compliance with legal requirements for clinical trials involving human subjects.

eligibility.text: Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group (ECOG) performance status of 0 or 1.
eligibility.justification: This criterion helps ensure patients are able to tolerate the study's demands and participate in the trial.

eligibility.text: Pathologically documented, locally advanced or metastatic KRAS G12V-mutated solid tumor malignancy.
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures patients are eligible for a treatment that targets the specific mutation.

eligibility.text: Received and progressed or been intolerant to prior standard therapy (including targeted therapy) appropriate for tumor type and stage.
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures patients have exhausted other treatment options, potentially impacting their ability to adhere to the study protocol.
This criterion helps ensure that the trial can detect a treatment effect by measuring tumor response.

eligibility.text: Adequate organ function (bone marrow, liver, kidney, coagulation)
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures patients have sufficient organ function to tolerate the study's demands and potential side effects of treatment.
This criterion helps ensure patient compliance with the study protocol by considering their ability to swallow medication.
This criterion ensures patients have a specific type of tumor that is being studied in the trial.

eligibility.text: Prior therapy with KRAS G12V inhibitor or direct RAS-targeted therapy (eg. degraders and/or inhibitors).
eligibility.justification: This criterion helps ensure patients have received prior treatment that may influence their response to the study drug.

eligibility.text: Any conditions that may affect the ability to take or absorb study drug.
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures patient compliance with the study protocol by considering potential medication interactions or absorption issues.

eligibility.text: Major surgery within 28 days prior to receiving study drug(s).
eligibility.justification: This criterion helps ensure patients have sufficient recovery time before starting the study medication.

eligibility.text: Patient is unable or unwilling to comply with protocol-required study visits or procedures.
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures patient safety by preventing participation in a trial if they are not able to follow the protocol.

power_analysis.assessment: N/A — Phase 1 trial, not powered for efficacy
